rounds.decision_reason: The staged candidate directly targets the observed binary-read, oversized-PDF, and interpreter-retry failure pattern while preserving prompt invariance.
The staged candidate removes the missing-`pypdf` startup trap and tightens mixed PDF/XLSX routing while preserving the invariant task prompt.

The staged candidate removes the oversized workbook stdout trap, adds a strict persisted-output recovery rule, and makes single-pass PDF-plus-XLSX diffing the default compare path without changing the invariant task prompt.
The staged candidate directly addresses the observed binary-read, oversized-PDF, interpreter-retry, and script-churn failure pattern while preserving prompt invariance.
The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly targets the remaining miss: summary-count drift after correct diff generation, while also trimming redundant mixed-skill startup guidance.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly targets the remaining inefficiency: dual-skill startup plus schema-assumption-first compare attempts that created a `KeyError` failure and an oversized diagnostic detour.
The staged candidate directly targets the observed binary-read, oversized-PDF, verbose-bootstrap, and redundant-reread failure pattern while preserving prompt invariance.
The staged candidate now makes `xlsx` the startup owner for mixed PDF/XLSX diffs, narrows PDF handling to bounded extraction, and preserves the invariant task prompt.
The staged candidate keeps the prompt invariant, preserves the successful workbook-led diff path, and closes the only local gap by preventing whole-report claims from bounded artifact reads.
The rewritten skillpack directly targets the observed binary-read, retrieval-heavy bootstrap, first-error fan-out, and redundant-reread failure pattern while preserving prompt invariance.
The rewritten skillpack directly targets the observed late-stage serialization rerun, mixed-skill startup ambiguity, and redundant post-success reread while preserving prompt invariance.

The staged skillpack now removes the conflicting mixed-artifact ownership signal that triggered dual-skill bootstrap and makes post-success exit explicit; the remaining packet-level misclassification is outside the writable skillpack.
The staged candidate directly addresses the wait-state failure by turning both skills into immediate-execution guides and requiring a concrete diff artifact before completion.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly targets the remaining inefficiencies: binary XLSX misrouting, oversized PDF first-read failures, and redundant second-pass extraction after a successful parse.

The next skillpack now front-loads binary and large-document routing, removes distracting tutorial bulk, and adds typed numeric normalization for the diff path, so it is worth advancing to the next round.

The staged candidate now directly targets the observed binary-XLSX read failure, oversized-PDF read failure, generic skill bootstrap, and unbounded preview behavior while preserving prompt invariance.
The staged candidate keeps the already successful PDF/XLSX diff path intact while removing the `pypdf`-only startup probe that produced the misleading transient failure signal.

The packet evidence points to an external heuristic mislabel rather than a broken executor path, so the staged candidate keeps behavior intact while trimming redundant guidance and making the artifact-complete stop state clearer.

The staged candidate directly targets the two front-loaded modality mistakes and the retry churn by forcing workbook-safe routing, bounded PDF first reads, and an immediate switch away from repeated mismatched Read attempts.

The session already demonstrated task correctness, and the staged candidate now removes corrupted PDF prompt text, trims generic cookbook bulk, and makes the one-pass compare workflow explicit for the next round.
The staged candidate keeps the prompt invariant and closes the two executor-side gaps the skillpack can actually affect: python3 launch discipline and suspicious-diff normalization checks.
The staged candidate removes the generic PDF bootstrap and adds a bounded, python3-first fillable-form workflow, so it is the right next skillpack to benchmark.

The staged candidate removes the logged `qpdf` dead-start and the remaining inspection-before-mapping drift while preserving the prompt-invariant form workflow, so it is the right round-2 skillpack to benchmark.

The staged candidate keeps the prompt-invariant form workflow but removes the remaining fillable-path read churn by forcing early `field_values.json` creation, bounded inventory inspection, and a one-pass unresolved fallback.
The staged candidate removes the over-guiding PDF bootstrap and replaces it with a bounded, python3-first court-form workflow, so it is the right next skillpack to benchmark.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant while directly targeting the remaining inefficiencies: it removes avoidable JSON-tool churn, gives SC-100 a bounded field map, and validates checkbox values before the first fill.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the remaining waste: it removes the avoidable bundled-script cwd miss and enforces an SC-100 single-audit stop rule after validation succeeds.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly attacks the observed waste: it removes the long generic handoff, forces the canonical page-level pypdf fallback, and adds a single-audit stop rule after a successful fill.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the remaining verifier risk: it converts SC-100 field discovery into one suffix-based pass, caps lookup churn after the first miss, and replaces routine page sweeps with one targeted audit.
The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed checkbox-repair loop plus the redundant post-fill verification loop with bounded local edits.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed inspect-before-fill failure, the ad hoc rewrite loop, and the missing bounded stop condition with local skillpack edits only.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly removes the three remaining sources of churn: redundant PDF bootstrap, a preventable extractor CLI retry, and a weak handoff from field inventory into map-fill-audit execution.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant but closes the remaining retrieval-heavy failure mode by making form filling enter through the skill, capping post-extraction inspection to one mapping pass, and forcing validated mappings directly into one fill and one audit.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed failures: it removes generic handbook bulk, makes bundled form scripts python3-first through one base-dir helper, and keeps fillable court forms on a bounded extract-once fast path with one targeted audit.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the remaining failure mode: it forces an early `field_values.json` commit, caps fillable discovery churn before rendering, and limits routine verification to one high-risk page.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed handoff stall by forcing immediate entry into `forms.md`, deleting the redundant form-start recipe, and requiring a filled PDF path or explicit blocker before termination.

The staged candidate removes the generic handbook bootstrap, adds python3-first recovery guardrails, and compresses court-form execution into a bounded classify-once fast path, so it is the right next skillpack to benchmark.

The staged candidate preserves the prompt, fixes the first-command cwd miss, forces `field_values.json` before fallback reads, and caps post-fill verification to a small page budget, so it is the right round-2 candidate to benchmark.

The staged candidate now lint-checks field mappings before fill and moves verification to structured text-first checks, which directly addresses the remaining round-2 procedural waste without changing the task prompt.
The staged next skillpack now removes the generic PDF bootstrap, adds a python3-first fillable fast path, and caps verification churn, so the next round should evaluate this tighter candidate.

The staged candidate now turns post-fill verification into a bounded exception path and removes the missing-directory retry in PDF image conversion, so the next round should evaluate this tighter skillpack.
The staged candidate now removes the cross-call interpreter-state assumption and caps fillable-path rereads, so the next round should test whether the PDF flow advances without the observed churn.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed waste: it blocks the first-write precondition miss, forces one gated build-verification path, and removes redundant post-build searching.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly targets the remaining failure mode: it removes extract-side branching and narrows post-`make descriptions` verification to one allowed secondary check with explicit environment-stop rules.

The staged candidate keeps the prompt invariant, hard-gates success on the required verifier, stops on environment-limited build failures, and replaces ad hoc syz-prog2c retries with one bounded smoke-test path.
The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant while adding the missing read-before-write, search-before-read, constants-ordering, and stop-after-make-descriptions guardrails.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly targets the remaining failure modes: it blocks shell-overwrite recovery after read-before-write errors, prunes unused flag groups before verification, and narrows verification to supported synchronous checks.

The staged candidate keeps the prompt invariant, hard-gates success on the allowlisted foreground verifier path, and narrows the constants flow to one bounded handoff after `make descriptions` passes.

rounds.decision_reason: The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and directly addresses the observed waste: it adds the missing read-before-write guard, removes the kernel-source/full-build constants detour, and narrows verification to `make descriptions` with an explicit environment-failure stop rule.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and converts the observed Docker compose bootstrap failure into a bounded `environment_blocked` stop path instead of allowing duplicate teardown or fallback-method churn.

The staged candidate stays prompt-invariant and now treats compose bootstrap failure as the terminal environment_blocked signal, suppresses redundant teardown retries after the same startup miss, and prevents constant-work branching before infra reachability is proven.
